description_long: This is a large, L-shaped wooden gate structure with a series of vertical wooden panels framed by dark horizontal and vertical beams. The top of the gate is capped with a corrugated metal or tiled roof, providing both weather protection and a distinct visual accent. The gate includes a main entry section and two perpendicular side extensions, forming a partial enclosure. The wood appears to be treated or stained, giving it a natural yet refined appearance, while the dark framing adds structural strength and contrast. There is a prominent post at one end, likely serving as a hinge or support for the main gate panel. The overall design is utilitarian with minimal decorative elements, suitable for urban or residential access control.
description: A large, L-shaped wooden gate with dark framing and a corrugated roof, featuring a main entry and side extensions for access control.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full L-shaped layout, the corrugated roof, and the arrangement of the main gate and side extensions.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the thickness of the panels and the separation between the main gate and side sections.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the length of the main gate section and the perpendicular side extensions with their roofs.
description_view_3: Reverse angled view focusing on the back of the gate and the supporting post structure.
attribute_surrounding_structure: wooden posts and framed wooden panels
